page: Amazon Web Services c9g.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by custom AWS Graviton ARM64 processors. It features 96 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, with a one-to-one mapping of vCPUs to physical cores due to a single thread per core configuration. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, ensuring minimal virtualization overhead and dedicated resource allocation.

The hardware configuration includes 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This ratio aligns with compute-intensive workloads that require high CPU capacity relative to memory footprint. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture and dedicated core design provide high compute density per resource unit. This makes the instance suitable for workloads such as high-performance computing, batch processing, media encoding, and distributed compute tasks that benefit from dedicated physical cores and high network throughput without requiring local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services c9g.24xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and powered by custom AWS Graviton ARM64 processors. The instance features 96 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core, ensuring dedicated CPU allocation. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware included. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps. This hardware profile is designed for compute-heavy workloads such as batch processing, distributed analytics, and media encoding that require high CPU density and substantial network throughput without local storage dependencies.
